title: phlegmariurus killipii
intro: Phlegmariurus killipii, also known as the Mexican climbing fern, is a species of plant in the lycopodiaceae family, characterized by its climbing habit and small, scale-like leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Phlegmariurus killipii are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory activities. Herbal preparations of Phlegmariurus killipii can be made by infusing the dried plant material in hot water to create a tea, which is said to provide relief from respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
